name: orders
title: Order Service
description: This API can be used for creating, updating, canceling and retrieving orders as well as archiving and retrieving archived orders related to a store.

schema.description: ID of the store that the order belongs to

schema.title: Order
schema.description: A order has to have at least 1 line item which is a representation of what the customer purchased

tax.title: Order Item Tax
tax.description: An Order Item tax can be applied at the level of an individual line item or shipping line

id.description: An identifier unique to the tax object (<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

externalIds.description: A set of externalIds associated with the tax object

items.title: External ID
items.description: An externalId object

type.description: Type of the externalId

value.description: Value of the externalId

referenceId.description: Reference identifier for the tax object

name.description: Name of the tax used for display purposes

amount.description: Money object with the tax amount
amount.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

ratePercentage.description: Rate of the tax when applied to the order. For example, `7.25` would represent a 7.25% tax rate

included.description: Determines whether the tax was included in order / line item / shipping subtotals

exempted.description: Determines whether the tax was exempted. Can be used to explicitly explain why a tax has a zero amount

metafields.description: Metafields that allow clients to store source data for a tax

items.title: Metafield
items.description: Metafields enable the consumer to attach additional information to a resource, such as an Order or Line Item

key.description: The unique identifier for the metafield in its namespace

type.description: The type of data that the metafield stores in the `value` field

value.description: The data to store in the metafield. The data is always stored as a string, regardless of the metafield's type

id.description: An identifier unique to the order (Order_<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

externalId.description: External identifier for the order. If provided, it must be a unique identifier at a channel level

number.description: The main number for the order. This is different from the `id` attribute, which is the `ID` of the order used by APIs. Uniqueness is not enforced. This number will show up in Order UIs to the merchant (C1).

numberDisplay.description: A human readable number handed out to customers (C2). Uniqueness is not enforced. For example at In-N-Out Burger, they assign an Order a `numberDisplay` from 1 - 100. Once the `numberDisplay` hits 100, the next Order has `numberDisplay` set to 1.

taxExempted.description: Details whether the order is tax exempt

owner.description: Owner creating the order in the order service. This is distinct from a channel. If not provided, the owner will be the Central Order Service

channelId.description: ID of the channel that the order belongs to

storeId.description: ID of the store that the order belongs to

businessId.description: ID of the business that the order belongs to

ventureId.description: ID of the venture that the order belongs to

statuses.description: Current fulfillment status, payment status, and main status for the order

paymentStatus.description: Possible payment statuses for an order
paymentStatus.title: Payment Status

fulfillmentStatus.description: Possible fulfillment statuses for an order
fulfillmentStatus.title: Fulfillment Status

totals.description: Grouped totals for an order
totals.title: Total

subTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of item subtotals for an order.
Formula: Sum of all lineItems[].totals.subTotal
subTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

shippingTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all shipping totals for an order.
Formula: Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.total
shippingTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

discountTotal.description: Sum of all discounts[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.discountTotal + Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.discountTotal
discountTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

productDiscountTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all product discounts for an order.
Formula: Sum of all discounts[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.discountTotal
Always non-positive (<=0)
productDiscountTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

shippingDiscountTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all shipping discounts for an order.
Formula: Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.discountTotal
Always non-positive (<=0)
shippingDiscountTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

feeTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all fees for an order.
Formula: Sum of all fees[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.feeTotal
feeTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

taxTotal.description: Sum of all taxes.filter(f => f.additional === true)[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.taxTotal + Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.taxTotal
taxTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

total.description:
Money object with the sum of all item, shipping, discount, fee, and tax totals.
Formula: subTotal + shippingTotal + feeTotal + taxTotal - discountTotal
total.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

customerId.description: Reference to a Customer as defined by the Connections service

cartId.description: ID associated with a cart. Used to link orders and carts for Inventory tracking purposes

address.description: The address associated with the billing info
address.title: Postal Address (Medium-Grained)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

firstName.description: First name associated with the billing info

lastName.description: Last name associated with the billing info

companyName.description: Company name associated with the billing info

phone.description: The phone number associated with the billing info

email.description: The email address associated with billing

quantity.description: Quantity purchased by the customer. Note this could be a float value (e.g. 2.3 Kgs)

returnQuantity.description: Sum of all return quantities for the line item

productId.description: Reference to a Product as defined by the V1 Catalog service

skuId.description: Reference to a SKU as defined by the V2 Catalog service

skuGroupId.description: Reference to a SKU Group entity as defined by the V2 Catalog service.

details.description: All detailed information for a line item - sku, selectedOptions, assetUrl, etc.

sku.description: SKU of the product. SKU is not required because some consumers do not make SKU a required field when adding a product

productAssetUrl.description: Product's associated asset URL. This can be an image thumbnail, video URL, etc.

unitOfMeasure.description: Product's unit of measure. Helps denote what was sold. If the line item purchased was grapes, the unit of measure could be set to `LBS`

items.description: A line item can have multiple selected add-ons

sku.description: SKU of the add-on. Certain systems set a SKU per add-on type

items.description: An add-on can have multiple values. For example a pizza with a Toppings add-on: `Mushrooms: +$0.00`, `Spinach: +$0.00`, and `Sausage: +$1.00`

name.description: Name of the add-on value

amount.description: Money object with the amount increase/decrease associated with the add-on value
amount.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

amountIncreased.description: Determines whether the add-on amount increases or decreases the price of the line item

costAdjustment.description: Cost increase/decrease associated with the add-on value. The value can be negative, unlike other amount fields across the Order schema
costAdjustment.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

items.description: A line item can have multiple selected options

values.description: Array of option values with string values

shipping.description: Shipping info associated to the line item. If not present, we assume the line item's shipping info is identical to the order's main shipping info
shipping.title: Shipping

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

serviceEndsAt.description: Datetime used to mark the end of service for a `STAY` or `SERVICE` line item

links.description: List of links associated to this order

items.title: Link Description
items.description: A request-related [HATEOAS link](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-hyperschema-02).

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

history.description: List of history events associated to this order

items.title: History event
items.description: Records a event that happened to the order

createdAt.description: The datetime when the history event was created in our system

inventoryAction.description: The action to take on inventory when the order is placed. Defaults to `AUTO_ADJUST`.
inventoryAction.title: Inventory Action

processedAt.description: The datetime when the order was processed/placed. If you're importing orders from an app or another platform, then you can set `processedAt` to a datetime in the past to match when the original order was created

archivedAt.description: The datetime when the order was archived

canceledAt.description: The datetime when the order was canceled

completedAt.description: The datetime when the order was completed

paidAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely paid

fulfilledAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely fulfilled

refundedAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely refunded

createdAt.description: The datetime when the order was created in our system

updatedAt.description: The datetime when the order was last updated in our system

orderVersion.description: Version identifier for the order. Changes whenever the order is updated.

ratePercentage.description: Rate of the tax when applied to the order. For example, `7.25` would represent a 7.25% tax rate

included.description: Determines whether the tax was included in order / line item / shipping subtotals

exempted.description: Determines whether the tax was exempted. Can be used to explicitly explain why a tax has a zero amount

metafields.description: Metafields that allow clients to store source data for a tax

items.title: Metafield
items.description: Metafields enable the consumer to attach additional information to a resource, such as an Order or Line Item

key.description: The unique identifier for the metafield in its namespace

type.description: The type of data that the metafield stores in the `value` field

value.description: The data to store in the metafield. The data is always stored as a string, regardless of the metafield's type

id.description: An identifier unique to the order (Order_<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

externalId.description: External identifier for the order. If provided, it must be a unique identifier at a channel level

number.description: The main number for the order. This is different from the `id` attribute, which is the `ID` of the order used by APIs. Uniqueness is not enforced. This number will show up in Order UIs to the merchant (C1).

numberDisplay.description: A human readable number handed out to customers (C2). Uniqueness is not enforced. For example at In-N-Out Burger, they assign an Order a `numberDisplay` from 1 - 100. Once the `numberDisplay` hits 100, the next Order has `numberDisplay` set to 1.

taxExempted.description: Details whether the order is tax exempt

owner.description: Owner creating the order in the order service. This is distinct from a channel. If not provided, the owner will be the Central Order Service

channelId.description: ID of the channel that the order belongs to

storeId.description: ID of the store that the order belongs to

businessId.description: ID of the business that the order belongs to

ventureId.description: ID of the venture that the order belongs to

statuses.description: Current fulfillment status, payment status, and main status for the order

paymentStatus.description: Possible payment statuses for an order
paymentStatus.title: Payment Status

fulfillmentStatus.description: Possible fulfillment statuses for an order
fulfillmentStatus.title: Fulfillment Status

totals.description: Grouped totals for an order
totals.title: Total

subTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of item subtotals for an order.
Formula: Sum of all lineItems[].totals.subTotal
subTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

shippingTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all shipping totals for an order.
Formula: Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.total

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. discountTotal.description: Sum of all discounts[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.discountTotal + Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.discountTotal

feeTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all fees for an order.
Formula: Sum of all fees[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.feeTotal
feeTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

taxTotal.description: Sum of all taxes.filter(f => f.additional === true)[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.taxTotal + Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.taxTotal

customerId.description: Reference to a Customer as defined by the Connections service

cartId.description: ID associated with a cart. Used to link orders and carts for Inventory tracking purposes

address.description: The address associated with the billing info
address.title: Postal Address (Medium-Grained)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

firstName.description: First name associated with the billing info

lastName.description: Last name associated with the billing info

companyName.description: Company name associated with the billing info

phone.description: The phone number associated with the billing info

email.description: The email address associated with billing

items.description: A line item can have multiple selected add-ons

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

serviceStartAt.description: Datetime used to mark the start of service for a `STAY` or `SERVICE` line item

serviceEndsAt.description: Datetime used to mark the end of service for a `STAY` or `SERVICE` line item

links.description: List of links associated to this order

items.title: Link Description
items.description: A request-related [HATEOAS link](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-hyperschema-02).

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

history.description: List of history events associated to this order

items.title: History event
items.description: Records a event that happened to the order

createdAt.description: The datetime when the history event was created in our system

transactionIds.description: List of transactions as defined by the Transaction Service associated with the order

items.description: Transaction ID

staffUserIds.description: Staff users associated with the order

items.description: Staff user identifier

tags.description: Tags associated to this order

items.description: A tag is an attribute of an order that does not change

deprecatedNote.description: Deprecated note field - use notes array instead

inventoryAction.description: The action to take on inventory when the order is placed. Defaults to `AUTO_ADJUST`.
inventoryAction.title: Inventory Action

processedAt.description: The datetime when the order was processed/placed. If you're importing orders from an app or another platform, then you can set `processedAt` to a datetime in the past to match when the original order was created

archivedAt.description: The datetime when the order was archived

canceledAt.description: The datetime when the order was canceled

completedAt.description: The datetime when the order was completed

paidAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely paid

fulfilledAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely fulfilled

refundedAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely refunded

createdAt.description: The datetime when the order was created in our system

updatedAt.description: The datetime when the order was last updated in our system

orderVersion.description: Version identifier for the order. Changes whenever the order is updated.

endpoints.description: Create an order with a specified identifier

schema.description: ID of the store that the order belongs to

requestBody.description: The order object to create with a passed in Global ID (e.g. `Order_<KSUID>`)

schema.title: Order
schema.description: A order has to have at least 1 line item which is a representation of what the customer purchased

tax.title: Order Item Tax
tax.description: An Order Item tax can be applied at the level of an individual line item or shipping line

id.description: An identifier unique to the tax object (<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

externalIds.description: A set of externalIds associated with the tax object

items.title: External ID
items.description: An externalId object

type.description: Type of the externalId

value.description: Value of the externalId

referenceId.description: Reference identifier for the tax object

name.description: Name of the tax used for display purposes

amount.description: Money object with the tax amount
amount.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

ratePercentage.description: Rate of the tax when applied to the order. For example, `7.25` would represent a 7.25% tax rate

included.description: Determines whether the tax was included in order / line item / shipping subtotals

exempted.description: Determines whether the tax was exempted. Can be used to explicitly explain why a tax has a zero amount

metafields.description: Metafields that allow clients to store source data for a tax

items.title: Metafield
items.description: Metafields enable the consumer to attach additional information to a resource, such as an Order or Line Item

key.description: The unique identifier for the metafield in its namespace

type.description: The type of data that the metafield stores in the `value` field

value.description: The data to store in the metafield. The data is always stored as a string, regardless of the metafield's type

id.description: An identifier unique to the order (Order_<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

externalId.description: External identifier for the order. If provided, it must be a unique identifier at a channel level

number.description: The main number for the order. This is different from the `id` attribute, which is the `ID` of the order used by APIs. Uniqueness is not enforced. This number will show up in Order UIs to the merchant (C1).

numberDisplay.description: A human readable number handed out to customers (C2). Uniqueness is not enforced. For example at In-N-Out Burger, they assign an Order a `numberDisplay` from 1 - 100. Once the `numberDisplay` hits 100, the next Order has `numberDisplay` set to 1.

taxExempted.description: Details whether the order is tax exempt

owner.description: Owner creating the order in the order service. This is distinct from a channel. If not provided, the owner will be the Central Order Service

channelId.description: ID of the channel that the order belongs to

storeId.description: ID of the store that the order belongs to

businessId.description: ID of the business that the order belongs to

ventureId.description: ID of the venture that the order belongs to

statuses.description: Current fulfillment status, payment status, and main status for the order

paymentStatus.description: Possible payment statuses for an order
paymentStatus.title: Payment Status

fulfillmentStatus.description: Possible fulfillment statuses for an order
fulfillmentStatus.title: Fulfillment Status

totals.description: Grouped totals for an order
totals.title: Total

subTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of item subtotals for an order.
Formula: Sum of all lineItems[].totals.subTotal
subTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

shippingTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all shipping totals for an order.
Formula: Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.total
shippingTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

discountTotal.description: Sum of all discounts[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.discountTotal + Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.discountTotal
discountTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

productDiscountTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all product discounts for an order.
Formula: Sum of all discounts[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.discountTotal
Always non-positive (<=0)
productDiscountTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

shippingDiscountTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all shipping discounts for an order.
Formula: Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.discountTotal
Always non-positive (<=0)
shippingDiscountTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

feeTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all fees for an order.
Formula: Sum of all fees[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.feeTotal
feeTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

taxTotal.description: Sum of all taxes.filter(f => f.additional === true)[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.taxTotal + Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.taxTotal
taxTotal.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

total.description:
Money object with the sum of all item, shipping, discount, fee, and tax totals.
Formula: subTotal + shippingTotal + feeTotal + taxTotal - discountTotal
total.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

customerId.description: Reference to a Customer as defined by the Connections service

cartId.description: ID associated with a cart. Used to link orders and carts for Inventory tracking purposes

address.description: The address associated with the billing info
address.title: Postal Address (Medium-Grained)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

firstName.description: First name associated with the billing info

lastName.description: Last name associated with the billing info

companyName.description: Company name associated with the billing info

phone.description: The phone number associated with the billing info

email.description: The email address associated with billing

For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

unitAmount.description: The base price of the line item including add-ons, but excluding taxes, fees, discounts. Deprecated and will become read-only in the future.
unitAmount.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

quantity.description: Quantity purchased by the customer. Note this could be a float value (e.g. 2.3 Kgs)

returnQuantity.description: Sum of all return quantities for the line item

productId.description: Reference to a Product as defined by the V1 Catalog service

skuId.description: Reference to a SKU as defined by the V2 Catalog service

skuGroupId.description: Reference to a SKU Group entity as defined by the V2 Catalog service.

details.description: All detailed information for a line item - sku, selectedOptions, assetUrl, etc.

sku.description: SKU of the product. SKU is not required because some consumers do not make SKU a required field when adding a product

productAssetUrl.description: Product's associated asset URL. This can be an image thumbnail, video URL, etc.

unitOfMeasure.description: Product's unit of measure. Helps denote what was sold. If the line item purchased was grapes, the unit of measure could be set to `LBS`

items.description: A line item can have multiple selected add-ons

sku.description: SKU of the add-on. Certain systems set a SKU per add-on type

items.description: An add-on can have multiple values. For example a pizza with a Toppings add-on: `Mushrooms: +$0.00`, `Spinach: +$0.00`, and `Sausage: +$1.00`

name.description: Name of the add-on value

amount.description: Money object with the amount increase/decrease associated with the add-on value
amount.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

amountIncreased.description: Determines whether the add-on amount increases or decreases the price of the line item

costAdjustment.description: Cost increase/decrease associated with the add-on value. The value can be negative, unlike other amount fields across the Order schema
costAdjustment.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

items.description: A line item can have multiple selected options

values.description: Array of option values with string values

shipping.description: Shipping info associated to the line item. If not present, we assume the line item's shipping info is identical to the order's main shipping info
shipping.title: Shipping

address.description: The address associated with the shipping info
address.title: Postal Address (Medium-Grained)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

firstName.description: First name associated with the shipping info

lastName.description: Last name associated with the shipping info

companyName.description: Company name associated with the shipping info

phone.description: The phone number associated with the shipping info

email.description: The email address associated with shipping

tags.description: Tags associated to this line item

items.description: A tag is an attribute of a line item that does not change

deprecatedNote.description: Single note field for backwards compatibility - use notes array for new implementations

serviceStartAt.description: Datetime used to mark the start of service for a `STAY` or `SERVICE` line item

serviceEndsAt.description: Datetime used to mark the end of service for a `STAY` or `SERVICE` line item

value.description: The data to store in the metafield. The data is always stored as a string, regardless of the metafield's type

links.description: List of links associated to this order

items.title: Link Description
items.description: A request-related [HATEOAS link](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-hyperschema-02).

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

history.description: List of history events associated to this order

items.title: History event
items.description: Records a event that happened to the order

createdAt.description: The datetime when the history event was created in our system

transactionIds.description: List of transactions as defined by the Transaction Service associated with the order

items.description: Transaction ID

staffUserIds.description: Staff users associated with the order

items.description: Staff user identifier

tags.description: Tags associated to this order

items.description: A tag is an attribute of an order that does not change

deprecatedNote.description: Deprecated note field - use notes array instead

inventoryAction.description: The action to take on inventory when the order is placed. Defaults to `AUTO_ADJUST`.
inventoryAction.title: Inventory Action

processedAt.description: The datetime when the order was processed/placed. If you're importing orders from an app or another platform, then you can set `processedAt` to a datetime in the past to match when the original order was created

archivedAt.description: The datetime when the order was archived

canceledAt.description: The datetime when the order was canceled

completedAt.description: The datetime when the order was completed

paidAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely paid

fulfilledAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely fulfilled

refundedAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely refunded

createdAt.description: The datetime when the order was created in our system

updatedAt.description: The datetime when the order was last updated in our system

orderVersion.description: Version identifier for the order. Changes whenever the order is updated.

order.title: Order
order.description: A order has to have at least 1 line item which is a representation of what the customer purchased

tax.title: Order Item Tax
tax.description: An Order Item tax can be applied at the level of an individual line item or shipping line

id.description: An identifier unique to the tax object (<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

externalIds.description: A set of externalIds associated with the tax object

items.title: External ID
items.description: An externalId object

type.description: Type of the externalId

value.description: Value of the externalId

referenceId.description: Reference identifier for the tax object

name.description: Name of the tax used for display purposes

amount.description: Money object with the tax amount
amount.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

ratePercentage.description: Rate of the tax when applied to the order. For example, `7.25` would represent a 7.25% tax rate

included.description: Determines whether the tax was included in order / line item / shipping subtotals

exempted.description: Determines whether the tax was exempted. Can be used to explicitly explain why a tax has a zero amount

metafields.description: Metafields that allow clients to store source data for a tax

items.title: Metafield
items.description: Metafields enable the consumer to attach additional information to a resource, such as an Order or Line Item

key.description: The unique identifier for the metafield in its namespace

type.description: The type of data that the metafield stores in the `value` field

value.description: The data to store in the metafield. The data is always stored as a string, regardless of the metafield's type

id.description: An identifier unique to the order (Order_<KSUID>). Auto-generated by the service on creation

externalId.description: External identifier for the order. If provided, it must be a unique identifier at a channel level

number.description: The main number for the order. This is different from the `id` attribute, which is the `ID` of the order used by APIs. Uniqueness is not enforced. This number will show up in Order UIs to the merchant (C1).

numberDisplay.description: A human readable number handed out to customers (C2). Uniqueness is not enforced. For example at In-N-Out Burger, they assign an Order a `numberDisplay` from 1 - 100. Once the `numberDisplay` hits 100, the next Order has `numberDisplay` set to 1.

taxExempted.description: Details whether the order is tax exempt

owner.description: Owner creating the order in the order service. This is distinct from a channel. If not provided, the owner will be the Central Order Service

channelId.description: ID of the channel that the order belongs to

storeId.description: ID of the store that the order belongs to

businessId.description: ID of the business that the order belongs to

ventureId.description: ID of the venture that the order belongs to

statuses.description: Current fulfillment status, payment status, and main status for the order

paymentStatus.description: Possible payment statuses for an order
paymentStatus.title: Payment Status

fulfillmentStatus.description: Possible fulfillment statuses for an order
fulfillmentStatus.title: Fulfillment Status

totals.description: Grouped totals for an order
totals.title: Total

Money object with the sum of item subtotals for an order.
Formula: Sum of all lineItems[].totals.subTotal

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

shippingTotal.description:
Money object with the sum of all shipping totals for an order.
Formula: Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.total

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

discountTotal.description: Sum of all discounts[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.discountTotal + Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.discountTotal

Money object with the sum of all fees for an order.

taxTotal.description: Sum of all taxes.filter(f => f.additional === true)[].amount + Sum of all lineItems[].totals.taxTotal + Sum of all shippingLines[].totals.taxTotal

customerId.description: Reference to a Customer as defined by the Connections service

cartId.description: ID associated with a cart. Used to link orders and carts for Inventory tracking purposes

address.description: The address associated with the billing info
address.title: Postal Address (Medium-Grained)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

firstName.description: First name associated with the billing info

lastName.description: Last name associated with the billing info

companyName.description: Company name associated with the billing info

phone.description: The phone number associated with the billing info

email.description: The email address associated with billing

sku.description: SKU of the product. SKU is not required because some consumers do not make SKU a required field when adding a product

productAssetUrl.description: Product's associated asset URL. This can be an image thumbnail, video URL, etc.

unitOfMeasure.description: Product's unit of measure. Helps denote what was sold. If the line item purchased was grapes, the unit of measure could be set to `LBS`

items.description: A line item can have multiple selected add-ons

sku.description: SKU of the add-on. Certain systems set a SKU per add-on type

items.description: An add-on can have multiple values. For example a pizza with a Toppings add-on: `Mushrooms: +$0.00`, `Spinach: +$0.00`, and `Sausage: +$1.00`

name.description: Name of the add-on value

amount.description: Money object with the amount increase/decrease associated with the add-on value
amount.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

amountIncreased.description: Determines whether the add-on amount increases or decreases the price of the line item

costAdjustment.description: Cost increase/decrease associated with the add-on value. The value can be negative, unlike other amount fields across the Order schema
costAdjustment.title: Simple Money with Fixed Precision

value.description: The value, which might represent intergrals for currencies like `JPY` that are not typically fractional; or, with an implied decimal fraction for currencies like `TND` that are subdivided into thousandths. For the implied number of decimal places for a currency code, see [ISO-4217 Currency Codes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_4217).

items.description: A line item can have multiple selected options

values.description: Array of option values with string values

shipping.description: Shipping info associated to the line item. If not present, we assume the line item's shipping info is identical to the order's main shipping info
shipping.title: Shipping

address.description: The address associated with the shipping info
address.title: Postal Address (Medium-Grained)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the address. For example, number and street name. For example, `3032 Bunker Hill Lane`. Required for compliance and risk checks. Must contain the full address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the address. For example, office suite or apartment number.

addressLine3.description: The third line of the address, if needed. For example, a street complement for Brazil; direction text, such as `next to Opera House`; or a landmark reference in an Indian address.

adminArea4.description: The neighborhood, ward, or district. Smaller than `adminArea3` or `subLocality`. For example, the postal sorting code that is used in Guernsey and many French territories, such as French Guiana; the fine-grained administrative levels in China.

adminArea3.description: A sub-locality, suburb, neighborhood, or district. Smaller than `adminArea2`. For example, in Brazil - Suburb, bairro, or neighborhood; in India - Sub-locality or district. Street name information may not always available and a sub-locality or district can reference a very small area.

adminArea2.description: A city, town, or village. Smaller than `adminArea1`.

adminArea1.description: The highest level sub-division in a country, which is usually a province, state, or ISO-3166-2 subdivision; formatted for postal delivery. For example, `CA` and not `California`. For example, for UK - A county; for USs - A state; for Canada - A province; for Japan - A prefecture; for Switzerland - A kanton.

postalCode.description: The postal code, which is the zip code or equivalent. Typically required for countries that have a postal code or an equivalent. See [Postal code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Postal_code).

countryCode.description: The [two-character ISO 3166-1 code](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1) that identifies the country or region. Note: The country code for Great Britain is `GB` and not `UK` as used in the top-level domain names for that country. Use country code `C2` for China for comparable uncontrolled price (CUP) method, bank-card, and cross-border transactions.

addressDetails.title: Address Details
addressDetails.description: The non-portable additional address details that are sometimes needed for compliance, risk, or other scenarios where fine-grain address information might be needed. Not portable with common third-party and open-source address libraries and redundant with core fields. For example, `address.addressLine1` is usually a combination of `addressDetails.streetNumber` and `streetName` and `streetType`.

streetName.description: The street name. Just `Drury` in `Drury Lane`.

streetType.description: The street type. For example, avenue, boulevard, road, or expressway.

deliveryService.description: The delivery service. Post office box, bag number, or post office name.

buildingName.description: A named locations that represents the premise. Usually a building name or number or collection of buildings with a common name or number. For example, Craven House.

subBuilding.description: The first-order entity below a named building or location that represents the sub-premise. Usually a single building within a collection of buildings with a common name. Can be a flat, story, floor, room, or apartment.

addressType.description: The type of address. Single character representation of a type. For example: 'B' for building, 'F' for organization, 'G' for general delivery, 'H' for high-rise, 'L' for large-volume organization, 'P' for Post Office box or delivery service, 'R' for rural route, 'S' for street, 'U' for unidentified address.

geoCoordinates.description: The latitude and longitude of the address. For example, `37.42242` and `-122.08585`.
geoCoordinates.title: Geographic Coordinates

firstName.description: First name associated with the shipping info

lastName.description: Last name associated with the shipping info

companyName.description: Company name associated with the shipping info

phone.description: The phone number associated with the shipping info

email.description: The email address associated with shipping

tags.description: Tags associated to this line item

items.description: A tag is an attribute of a line item that does not change

deprecatedNote.description: Single note field for backwards compatibility - use notes array for new implementations

serviceStartAt.description: Datetime used to mark the start of service for a `STAY` or `SERVICE` line item

serviceEndsAt.description: Datetime used to mark the end of service for a `STAY` or `SERVICE` line item

links.description: List of links associated to this order

items.title: Link Description
items.description: A request-related [HATEOAS link](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-handrews-json-schema-hyperschema-02).

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

tags.description: Tags associated to this order

deprecatedNote.description: Deprecated note field - use notes array instead

inventoryAction.description: The action to take on inventory when the order is placed. Defaults to `AUTO_ADJUST`.

processedAt.description: The datetime when the order was processed/placed. If you're importing orders from an app or another platform, then you can set `processedAt` to a datetime in the past to match when the original order was created

archivedAt.description: The datetime when the order was archived

canceledAt.description: The datetime when the order was canceled

completedAt.description: The datetime when the order was completed

paidAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely paid

fulfilledAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely fulfilled

refundedAt.description: The datetime when the order was completely refunded

createdAt.description: The datetime when the order was created in our system

updatedAt.description: The datetime when the order was last updated in our system

orderVersion.description: Version identifier for the order. Changes whenever the order is updated.

parameters.description: Direction indicator used for cursor based pagination
